title: Add skopeo rpm spec file to contrib
body:
Adding skopeo spec file to repo to provide public access to files required to build rpm. Location in repo follows buildah's existing convention/location.

Spec file is from the srpm from the following repo with the _commitid_ changed to REPLACEWITHCOMMITID to match buildah .spec convention:
https://cbs.centos.org/repos/virt7-container-common-candidate/source/SRPMS/

Please advise if there is an updated version available or if there is an alternate location to access the spec.

Signed-off-by: pixdrift <support@pixeldrift.net>

comments.body:
@lsm5 @baude PTAL
Should we setup the autobuild like we do for buildah and podman?
SGTM and LGTM, but will leave the approval to @lsm5 and @baude.  @mtrmac any angst with this?

Is there a possibility of getting the same path/file convention for spec files used in all projectatomic git repos. Not necessarily `this` structure, but one that is the same for all?

I checked libpod which has it under `/contrib/spec/podman.spec.in`. I didn't want to push my luck and suggest a move in that repo too 😄
I think it would be a good idea to get them all the same basic path.

@rhatdan RE: the daily autobuild for fedora rawhide, it happens on my daily-rebuild repo. See: https://travis-ci.org/lsm5/daily-rebuild/builds . It uses the fedora dist-git repos directly and doesn't depend on spec files included upstream. skopeo is included in the current daily rebuilds already. In case you need this spec file as a general pointer to `ohh how do i quickly build this?`, then LGTM.

I'll leave it to @baude to decide if the COPR build stuff he does for podman should be done here as well.

I’m not _fundamentally_ against having a spec file in the repo (and if the buildah/podman/… universe wants to standardize on a convention (@baude?), fine); my primary concern is: will anyone maintain it, or at least notice that it is broken?

What do you actually need to _do_ with the spec file? It’s not quite clear to me from
>  to provide public access to files required to build rpm

who would benefit and how. The Fedora dist-git is just as public, for example.

Fedora is pretty clear in https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Packaging:Guidelines#Spec_Maintenance_and_Canonicity that Fedora’s spec files are maintained in Fedora’s git; if they get out of sync, the Fedora git version is the canonical one.

This is not just academic: every time Fedora’s git bumps the `Release:` version, the spec in this repo should bump it as well, possibly to a higher number, so that the locally-built RPM is actually used. So, a Fedora-targeted spec file in here would be a hassle to maintain.

---

Another aspect is that the Fedora `skopeo` SRPM contains also the `skopeo-containers` subpackage, which does not really have much to do with skopeo itself — e.g. note the `Source1: storage.conf` source file.  If the SRPM from this repo is supposed to be drop-in-compatible, we would need to maintain copies of those fairly unrelated files here as well.

---

> @rhatdan RE: the daily autobuild for fedora rawhide, it happens on my daily-rebuild repo. See: https://travis-ci.org/lsm5/daily-rebuild/builds . It uses the fedora dist-git repos directly and doesn't depend on spec files included upstream.

Yeah, using the Fedora spec directly seems, at least for Fedora, a better approach.

_Maybe_ we should just mention in `README.md` that various distributions, like Fedora, maintain a `skopeo` package and building from source may be unnecessary? I don’t know, overall I am probably missing the point of this PR.

On Mon, Apr 30, 2018 at 10:13:06AM -0700, Miloslav Trmač wrote:
> Another aspect is that the Fedora `skopeo` SRPM contains also the `skopeo-containers` subpackage, which does not really have much to do with skopeo itself — e.g. note the `Source1: storage.conf` source file.  If the SRPM from this repo is supposed to be drop-in-compatible, we would need to maintain copies of those fairly unrelated files here as well.

This file can be upstramed, I hope.

-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5
mounts.conf and registries.conf are distro centric.  So we should probably provide empty templates.

I have been thinking we should rename skopeo-containers to something like containers-config or something along those lines.  Since that is really what the package contains.  mounts.
```
rpm -q  skopeo-containers -l
/etc/containers
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.d
/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml
/etc/containers/storage.conf
/usr/share/containers
/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf
/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/rhel/secrets
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/etc-pki-entitlement
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhel7.repo
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhsm
/var/lib/atomic/sigstore
```
Even the /var/lib/atomic directory should be changed....  The secrets stuff is obviously not something SUSE would be interested in.  :^)

@rhatdan, as an educational exercise for myself, what is the current source of the `mounts.conf` that is referenced in the skopeo .spec file? ie. where can I go to find the source of `mounts.conf` that is used in the RH shipped rpm?

Is it worth mentioning here the `registries.conf` file from atomic-registries moving into skopeo-containers? which was discussed here:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/635

Would that involve moving the `registries.conf` into this repo? or would that file be included in the skopeo-container rpm spec file similar to `mounts.conf`?

Added the `mounts.conf` file to contrib with the other files described in the .spec so this file is available if this PR is accepted. Will need to update to a more generic version that isn't distro specific as described by @rhatdan above.

Currently mounts.conf and registries.conf is stored with the the spec file in the internal koji repo.  We could setup different contrib files under skopeo for each distribution.  Most of the Red Hat ones are just sorting the order of registries.conf and we have the RHEL secrets in fedora and Centos, since there is nothing stopping a user from using a RHEL subscription to develop RHEL containers while sitting on a Fedora or Centos Box.

Making these more public would probably be a good idea.

storage.conf is also customizable by the distros although I think there would be more concensus there.

What do you think about changing the subpackage name to containers-config.

Using 'common' is a fairly standard convention in other RHEL packages, could `containers-common` work as a package name which also then doesn't limit it to configuration files/data? This may mask the intent if the package is just for config files though 😄

@rhatdan, looking at the src rpm the `registries.conf` comes from here, which has distro specific version for fedora and rhel (and a generic example too):
https://github.com/projectatomic/registries
I am fine with common.

@rhatdan, where to from here? is the plan to put `containers-common` in a new projectatomic repo with its own spec? If so, I can update this PR to remove the files that will be moved into `containers-common`.

For now I would just leave it here.  I was just thinking we would rename the skopeo-containers subpackage to contianers-commont.  If we were to setup a new repo, I would just do it in github.com/containers/common

So the rename is fine and the spec builds cleanly, but the impact of the package name change from `skopeo-containers` to `containers-common` is far and wide.

So far I have identified the following packages that have `Requires` on `skopeo-containers`
```
buildah
podman
docker-common (which was a surprise)
```

How is this kind of effort usually co-ordinated?

You should add a 
provides: skopeo-containers

Then we can slowly fix the other packages to require the new name.
@rhatdan Cheers, thanks for the tip.

Upgrade  of containers-common looks good/clean, running into this strange issue updating skopeo though relating to epoch (the required version of containers-common that matches skopeo version is installed, but yum doesn't like it because of epoch value):
```
Error: Package: 1:skopeo-0.1.27-1.git7159f56.el7.x86_64 (/skopeo-0.1.27-1.git7159f56.el7.x86_64)
           Requires: containers-common = 0.1.27-1.git7159f56.el7
           Installed: 1:containers-common-0.1.27-1.git7159f56.el7.x86_64 (installed)
               containers-common = 1:0.1.27-1.git7159f56.el7
```

Do we need to also adda 
obsoletes: skopeo-containers

It's already in there:
https://github.com/pixdrift/skopeo/blob/master/contrib/rpm/skopeo.spec#L184-L185

-edit-

Added version specific `Provides` to the `containers-common` subpackage which has resolved it. Keen for someone to look over it. All looks ok in testing upgrade.

It looks like the Epoch is causing some minor issues with dependencies.. is anyone able to give some background/insight into this? It's only enabled for CentOS (which is what I am building on).
https://github.com/pixdrift/skopeo/blob/master/contrib/rpm/skopeo.spec#L37-L39

CI failure looks unrelated.

Found reference to the Epoch configuration here, looking into it:
```
* Sat Jul 01 2017 Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org> - 0.1.22-2.dev.git5d24b67
- Epoch: 1 for CentOS as CentOS Extras' build already has epoch set to 1
```

Resulting packages built on CentOS 7.4 are here:
http://rpmbuild.pixeldrift.net/scratch/

Update was clean from the packaged versions in CentOS Extras. If anyone can test the spec on Fedora it would be appreciated. Any other feedback welcome.
@lsm5 PTAL
All good. Thanks for the feedback/advice re: source of specs, I will close this out.
